terms.definition: A definite loop with initialization, condition, and update in one header; ideal for counter-controlled repetition.
An indefinite loop that repeats while a boolean condition remains true; condition checked before each iteration.
A loop that executes the body at least once, then repeats while the condition remains true.
terms.example: do { input = readInt("Enter (0 to quit): "); } while (input != 0);

terms.definition: Also called for-each; iterates over elements of arrays/collections without explicit indices.
One execution of the loop body.
A variable that tracks the current iteration number or position.
A variable that aggregates results across iterations (sum, product, string build).
terms.example: int sum = 0; for (int x : arr) { sum += x; }

terms.definition: A special value signaling the end of input or termination of a loop.
The boolean expression controlling whether the loop continues.
The block of statements executed each iteration.
A loop inside another loop, often for 2D traversal or pattern generation.
A boundary mistake causing one too few or too many iterations.
terms.example: for (int i = 0; i <= arr.length; i++) { /* i == arr.length crashes */ }

terms.definition: A loop that never terminates because the condition never becomes false or there is no exit.
Immediately exits the nearest enclosing loop or switch.
Skips the rest of the current iteration and jumps to the next condition check.
